records.name: Final-General-Specifications-for-Roads-and-Bridges_March-2026.pdf
records.sample_text: MINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT THE REPUBLIC OF UGANDA General Specifications for Road and Bridge Works Ministry of Works and Transport Plot 57 -59, Old Port Bell Road PO BOX 7174, Kampala, Uganda MARCH 2026 i FOREWORD By the powers entrusted to me under the Roads Act 2019, I am pleased to present the General Specifications for Road and Bridge Works, 202 6. As Government, we are committed to enhancing the quality and safety of Uganda ’s road infrastructure. The General Specifications will be part of the legal and technical framework for use in planning, design, construction, maintenance and operation of efficient and effective public road and bridge infrastructure in our country. The Ministry of Works and Transport, in consultation with stakeholders has undertaken a comprehensive review of the previous specifications that were launched in 2005 and updated them accordingly. The revisions have incorporated the latest advancements in technology, the best pract ices observed locally, regionally and internationally, as well as the valuable experiences gathered from practitioners in the road construction industry. The importance of well -designed, constructed and maintained public roads cannot be overstated. They ar e vital to our national development and facilitate trade, tourism, security, agriculture, mining, mobility, and connectivity across our

records.sample_text: Non-Motorized Transport Manual Non-Motorized Transport Manual aMINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT THE REPUBLIC OF UGANDA MINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT ROAD DESIGN MANUAL VOLUME VII: NON-MOTORIZED TRANSPORT JANUARY 2025 b Non-Motorized Transport Manual Non-Motorized Transport Manual MINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT MINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT JANUARY 2025 Non-Motorized Transport Manual Non-Motorized Transport Manual cMINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT THE REPUBLIC OF UGANDA MINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT ROAD DESIGN MANUAL VOLUME VII: NON-MOTORIZED TRANSPORT JANUARY 2025 d Non-Motorized Transport Manual Non-Motorized Transport Manual MINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT Non-Motorized Transport Manual Non-Motorized Transport Manual iMINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT Foreword By the powers vested in me under Section 6 of the Road Act (2019), I am honoured to present the Non-Motorized Transport Manual, 2025. This manual will be used as one of the standards for the safe, efficient, effective and sustainable design of public roads in Uganda. As a Ministry, we remain steadfast in our commitment to integrating pedestrians and cycling facilities into design and construction of road and bridge infrastructure. records.name: URDM-Manual-Part-1-July-2023.pdf
records.sample_text: The Republic of Uganda MINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT ROAD DESIGN MANUAL Volume VI: Urban Roads PART 1: Design Manual and Appendices FINAL JULY 2023 URBAN ROADS DESIGN MANUAL July 2023 MINISTRY OF WORKS AND TRANSPORT i FOREWORD Background and Objective of the updated 2023 Urban Roads Design Manual Road transport is the dominant mode of transport in Uganda, presently accounting for over 90 percent of the volume of freight and passenger movement. Uganda has a road network of approximately 159,529km of national, district, urban and community access roads, representing one of the huge national assets, valued at approx. US$ 7.7M, in 2019. Presently, the roads network is largely unpaved with only about 7,149km of the roads paved, thereby representing 4.5% of the total road network. Uganda’s roads network is a critical component for the socio-economic development of the country, as it is vital for people and freight transport that supports production, competitiveness, trade and mobility. Road network development and maintenance are key strategic priorities for national and human development. In the last 10 years, the numbers of Cities, Municipals and Town Councils have risen rapidly due to creation of new districts, cities and other local government administrative units.
